program.name: Woodside Graduate and Student Pathways (Graduate Development Program and student programs)
program.salary_text: Australia: The Summer Vacation Program is a paid work experience opportunity, with accommodation and return travel provided to applicants residing outside of Perth. USA: The Summer Internship Program is a paid work experience opportunity, and accommodation and return travel are provided if applicable.
program.duration_text: Summer Vacation Program (Australia): 12-week program. Indigenous Summer Vacation Program (Australia): full-time for 6–12 weeks. Summer Internship Program (USA): minimum of twelve (12) consecutive weeks during Summer 2026. Graduate Development Program (Australia and USA): three years of structured development.
program.rotation_text: Graduate Development Program (Australia and USA): three years of structured development with carefully designed job rotations, plus a site visit in the first year of the program.

eligibility.work_rights: Australia (Summer Vacation Program and Graduate Development Program): Australian or New Zealand citizen, or Australian permanent resident; or an international student currently studying (and for SVP also residing) in Australia with unrestricted Australian working rights over the relevant period. USA (Summer Internship Program and Graduate Development Program): must be authorized to work in the United States and must not require current or future sponsorship for employment visa status (for example, F-1). Timor-Leste (Professional Development Program): must be a Timor-Leste national.
eligibility.graduation_window: Australia (Graduate Development Program): final year of university or up to two years post-university graduation. Australia (Summer Vacation Program): penultimate (second to last) or final year of university. USA (Summer Internship Program): rising junior or senior with an expected graduation date of Spring 2027 through Spring 2028. USA (Graduate Development Program): final year of university or up to two years post-university graduation. Better Future Pathways (Australia): any year level besides penultimate or final year.
minimum_requirements: Australia (Graduate Development Program): Weighted Average Mark (WAM) of 65 or above in your most recent studies.
USA (Summer Internship Program and Graduate Development Program): minimum cumulative GPA of 3.30.
USA (Summer Internship Program): enrolled in an accredited college or university for the full duration of the Summer 2026 internship.
USA (Summer Internship Program): available for a minimum of twelve (12) consecutive weeks during Summer 2026.
Timor-Leste (Professional Development Program): enrolled full-time in an undergraduate or postgraduate degree course at a university.
Timor-Leste (Professional Development Program): demonstrated workplace English language competency.

stages.name: Find out what area of the business best suits you
stages.details: Use Woodside’s degree matcher to find the best business area for you.

stages.name: Complete the pathways quiz
stages.details: The pathways quiz shows what you are eligible to apply for.

stages.name: Online application
stages.details: The quiz provides a link to apply for the role best suited for you.

stages.name: Online interview assessment
stages.details: You may be invited to complete an online interview assessment via a link emailed to you.

stages.name: Formal interview
stages.details: If successful, you will be invited to an in-person or video interview.

stages.name: Due diligence checks
stages.details: May include a national criminal record check, sanctions check, academic record/qualification check, and reference checking.

stages.name: Offer
stages.details: If successful, a Talent Acquisition team member contacts you to extend a formal offer.

exec_themes: Operational excellence in core assets (safety, reliability, efficiency, cost control) to protect cash flow.
Disciplined capital allocation and project delivery to grow the portfolio while maintaining an investment‑grade balance sheet.
Shareholder returns focus, especially dividends within a stated payout framework.
Growth led by LNG and major projects (e.g., Scarborough, Trion, Louisiana LNG) alongside selective acquisitions/partnering.
“Thriving through the energy transition” by integrating climate targets into overall strategy (Scope 1–2 reduction plus investment in transition products/services).
Customer-led approach (sell what customers will buy) across both traditional energy and newer energy offerings.
Portfolio streamlining to prioritise assets/activities expected to deliver the most value (including slowing some new energy work and reducing exploration).
commercial_context.profit_engine: Woodside’s profit engine is producing and selling oil and gas (especially LNG) from a “reliable and cost competitive base business,” using high asset reliability and low unit costs to generate cash flow, then recycling that cash into major growth projects and paying dividends (target payout ratio framework).
commercial_context.headwinds: Woodside flags macro and market uncertainty as key headwinds: volatile commodity prices; inflationary pressures; tariffs/trade restrictions that raise supply-chain cost and complexity; geopolitical tensions; and energy-transition regulatory/market uncertainty that could change hydrocarbon demand and push faster portfolio diversification. It also highlights project delivery and safety risks in major construction activity.
commercial_context.esg: Woodside’s climate/ESG focus is centered on reducing net equity Scope 1 and 2 emissions (targets: 15% by 2025 and 30% by 2030, with an aspiration of net zero by 2050 or sooner), using a hierarchy of “avoid” and “reduce” emissions (design-out/operate-out) and then “offset” remaining emissions with carbon credits. It also positions methane management as a priority (including joining OGMP 2.0 in 2024 and preparing an inaugural submission in 2025). For Scope 3, Woodside states an approach of investing in products and services for the energy transition (US$5B by 2030 target) and an abatement-capacity target linked to taking FID on new energy/lower-carbon opportunities by 2030.
commercial_context.recent_pivot: Recent strategic moves described by Woodside include: approving the Louisiana LNG development (positioning to scale global LNG), elevating the link between executive remuneration and climate/new energy progress from 2024, and tightening portfolio focus (slowing work on H2OK after acquiring Beaumont New Ammonia; reducing exploration activity; streamlining the asset base and prioritising operated LNG assets, including via an Australian asset swap agreement with Chevron).

notes: The page states that Australian Summer Vacation and Graduate roles are 'opening 1 August 2026'.
No close date for applications is stated on the page.

sources.title: Woodside Graduate Programs & Summer Internships | Apply Now

notes: Eligibility rules vary by country and by pathway; only requirements explicitly listed on the page are included.
No specific eligible disciplines list is explicitly provided on this page (it references a degree matcher tool instead).

notes: The recruitment stages are presented as the 'Australian pathway process' on the page.

sources.title: Woodside Graduate Programs & Summer Internships | Apply Now

notes: Some elements (e.g., detailed financial mechanics beyond dividends/cost focus, or a full list of strategic priorities) may be described more comprehensively in the 2024 Annual Report PDF, but that PDF could not be fetched by the tool (HTTP error) during this run. Summary therefore relies on transcripts, investor pages, and sustainability/climate pages available on woodside.com.

‘Recent pivot’ is based on management’s own descriptions of changes in emphasis (e.g., slowing certain work, reducing exploration) and major approvals; Woodside does not label these explicitly as a single ‘pivot’ section, so phrasing is synthesized from multiple official statements without adding new facts.
